name: Carbon Trace
what_it_is: An award-winning immersive memoir about fighting to become an engineer when the world wasn't built for you to.
why_it_exists: Turns lived experience with gender bias into something you feel, not just read.
long_description: A browser-native narrative experience built on Canvas 2D, PixiJS displacement, and GSAP-timed scenes, scored with layered Howler audio. The diamond — pressure into permanence — drives every transition through bias, endurance, and becoming.
outcome: WeCoded 2026 winner. A deployed, immersive impact piece that turns a personal story into something audiences feel and remember.
image_alt: Carbon Trace visual experience following a diamond metaphor through pressure, bias, and transformation toward becoming

blog_posts.title: Forged Between Coal and Code

name: Unearthed
what_it_is: An award-winning tool that walks the energy supply chain backward, from any U.S. address to the coal mine feeding its grid.
why_it_exists: Names the exact mine — operator, accidents, emissions — behind every light switch.
long_description: SvelteKit and FastAPI on Cloud Run trace outlet to power plant to fuel contract to mine, surfacing MSHA accident records, EIA production, and EPA emissions for each. Snowflake Cortex powers natural-language queries; a live tonnage ticker shows extraction in real time.
outcome: Earth Day 2026 winner. A deployed tool naming the mine behind any U.S. address, backed by federal MSHA, EIA, and EPA data.
image_alt: Unearthed application interface tracing electricity from a user's address to the coal mine feeding their grid

blog_posts.title: Unearthed—The Coal Mine Behind Every Light Switch

name: Save the Sun
what_it_is: A deduction game where you out-question an Oracle and race a Gemini-played wolf to name the hidden rune first.
why_it_exists: Proves an AI can be a live, fallible opponent without ever being trusted with the truth.
long_description: A SvelteKit app on Cloud Run where a deterministic engine owns every fact while Gemini interprets Asks, plays the wolf, transcribes speech, and voices both characters — never seeing the secret. Provably winnable every round, keyboard- and screen-reader-complete.
outcome: Shipped and deployed live — spoken Oracle and wolf, plus a debug stream proving the engine owns truth. A Gemini + Ode to Turing entry in the June Solstice Game Jam 2026.
image_alt: A rune stone blazing with golden light at sunrise while Sköll, the great wolf, watches from a dark ridge.

blog_posts.title: The Oracle and the Wolf — I Made Gemini Lose Like a Kid

name: Vestige
what_it_is: An on-device ADHD brain tracker for Android: 30-second voice entries in, sourced behavioral patterns out. No grading, no gamification.
why_it_exists: On-device ADHD recall, built so nothing — not even your voice — ever leaves the phone.
long_description: Gemma 4 E4B takes native audio (no SpeechRecognizer) and streams transcription plus follow-up, then three background lens passes feed a deterministic Kotlin convergence resolver. EmbeddingGemma drives tone-word drift; a sealed NetworkGate and a verifyNoTelemetry Gradle task make privacy a build-time guarantee, not a promise.
outcome: Gemma 4 Challenge entry. 73% meaningful lens divergence against a 50% bar; 12/12 entries parsed at a 21.2s mean on a Galaxy S24 Ultra.
image_alt: Vestige Android brain tracker surfacing sourced behavioral patterns from on-device Gemma 4 analysis

blog_posts.title: Vestige: A Gemma 4 Brain Tracker That Won't Blow Smoke Up Your Ass

name: Legacy Smelter
what_it_is: A satirical incident-management app where Hotfix the dragon roasts your worst code screenshots — and thermally decommissions them.
why_it_exists: Upload cursed code; a dragon files the incident report and smelts it to slag.
long_description: React 19 and Tailwind v4 front a PixiJS-animated dragon and Howler audio, while Gemini Flash generates deadpan enterprise incident reports. Each report joins a global manifest where the community escalates a public leaderboard of P0 disasters.
outcome: A live, shareable joke product — AI-generated reports, animated remediation, and community escalation in one polished challenge build.
image_alt: Hotfix the dragon smelting an uploaded legacy-code screenshot into molten slag while generating a structured incident report

blog_posts.title: Meet Hotfix—The Dragon Your Legacy Code Deserves

name: System Notes
what_it_is: An AI-assembled portfolio site built to test orchestration and authorship boundaries under human supervision.
why_it_exists: A portfolio that delegates its own construction to AI — and logs every decision.
long_description: Rather than hand-author a portfolio, I scoped intent and constraints and let Gemini 3 Pro and Antigravity assemble structure and content. The artifact isn't the site — it's the traceable record of the decisions an AI makes building a system on a leash.
outcome: A read-only, AI-assembled site that documents its own decision boundaries instead of curating a narrative.
image_alt: System Notes project dashboard showing AI-generated portfolio entries and architecture decisions

blog_posts.title: I Stopped Reviewing Code: A Backend Dev's Experiment with Google Gemini
Conversational Retrieval: When Chat Becomes Navigation
From Static Portfolio to Indexed Decisions
My Portfolio Doesn't Live on the Page

name: SupaScribe Notes
what_it_is: A lightweight pipeline that turns messy thoughts into structured System Notes index cards, stored durably in Supabase.
why_it_exists: Freeform thoughts in, schema-perfect index cards out — no cleanup step.
long_description: Exposed as an MCP server so any AI client can write directly: Supabase Edge Functions enforce schema at capture time, not cleanup time. Freeform input goes in, structured System Notes cards come out, and Postgres keeps them. Opinionated by design — no freeform persistence, no exceptions.
outcome: An opinionated capture-to-storage pipeline that trades flexibility for guaranteed schema adherence.
image_alt: SupaScribe Notes app interface showing structured note card generation powered by AI and Supabase

blog_posts.title: I Let AI Write to My Database (With Guardrails)🔬

name: DEV Community Dashboard
what_it_is: A public signal-triage dashboard that routes volunteer attention to the DEV posts the feed would otherwise bury.
why_it_exists: Surfaces the posts where a thoughtful human reply actually matters most.
long_description: Built on the Forem API with Node and TypeScript on Cloud Run, it scores posts by engagement and conversational signal — not recency — using LLM analysis to flag high-need, low-interaction questions before they quietly disappear beneath higher-performing content.
outcome: A deployed triage layer that helps volunteers answer unanswered questions and emerging discussions faster.
image_alt: DEV Community Dashboard interface showing prioritized posts and signal-based interaction metrics

blog_posts.title: Find the DEV Post That Needs You Now 🫶

name: Awesome Copilot
what_it_is: A curated, opinionated set of GitHub Copilot workflows, agents, and patterns that earned their place.
why_it_exists: The Copilot workflows that survived daily use — not the ones that just sounded clever.
long_description: Not a neutral list. I build a lot of Copilot tooling locally and most of it dies there; only what survives daily use, frustration, and edge cases ships here. It reflects how I actually work with Copilot, not how the docs say I should.
outcome: A widely adopted resource that favors practical utility over volume, maintained by a GitHub Copilot–certified author.
image_alt: Awesome GitHub Copilot repository banner listing curated AI workflows and agent patterns

blog_posts.title: Troubleshooting Production with GitHub Copilot
Oh I Have Big News—I'm GitHub Copilot Certified!
Everything I Know About GitHub Copilot Instructions
GitHub Copilot Chat Modes Explained with Personality
Leash not Autopilot: Building Predictable AI Behavior with Copilot Instructions

name: Dev.to Mirror
what_it_is: An automated, AI-optimized mirror of my technical writing.
why_it_exists: Makes long-form technical writing legible to the AI systems that index it.
long_description: A Python job on GitHub Actions pulls my DEV posts, restructures them for AI ingestion, and deploys to GitHub Pages. Not SEO or analytics — it exists so AI systems can correctly index and cite the work.
outcome: Improved AI discoverability with zero ongoing maintenance.
image_alt: Dev.to Mirror workflow diagram showing automated syncing of technical blogs for AI ingestion

blog_posts.title: Weekend Hack: Making My Blog AI Searchable (No Flames Required)

name: RAI Lint
what_it_is: A published linter that hard-enforces AI attribution directly in commit workflows.
why_it_exists: Makes AI attribution in commits explicit and enforceable — not implied and ignored.
long_description: TypeScript and Python rules plug into commitlint and gitlint to require explicit attribution whenever AI tools touch a commit. Strict and mechanical by design — traceability, not policy theater. Licensed under PolyForm Shield.
outcome: Live adoption that turns invisible AI assistance into explicit, auditable git history.
image_alt: RAI Lint banner enforcing explicit AI attribution in git commits and workflows

blog_posts.title: Is GitHub Copilot Safe? The Fun and Hard Truth About Responsible AI
Did AI Erase Attribution? Your Git History is Missing a Co-Author
Signing Your Name on AI-Assisted Commits with RAI Footers

name: Hermes Agent
what_it_is: A personal AI-agent playground exploring memory as a first-class concern.
why_it_exists: A zero-stakes sandbox for getting agent memory wrong on purpose.
long_description: Python, Ollama, and Postgres back a local agent built to watch how memory behaves when it exists, evolves, and occasionally misbehaves — without production pressure or polish shaping the decisions.
outcome: A working local sandbox whose memory experiments inform future agent design.
image_alt: Hermes Agent interface showing local AI memory experiments and conversational history

name: Delegate Action
what_it_is: A temporary AI coding agent shipped as a GitHub Action.
why_it_exists: AI-generated PRs under my own credentials, while the platform caught up.
long_description: Built to generate draft PRs under my own PAT — with explicit human review and ownership — until Copilot's coding agent supported user PAT tokens via CLI in Actions. A deliberate stopgap with injection detection baked in.
outcome: Outpaced by Copilot's native coding agent before it proved out — a deliberate stopgap that mapped the auth and injection patterns it was built to test.
image_alt: Delegate Action GitHub workflow allowing secure AI-generated pull requests via personal access tokens

name: Underfoot Travel
what_it_is: Narrative travel discovery turned into an experiment in AI orchestration and parallel workflows.
why_it_exists: A hackathon travel app repurposed into an AI-orchestration testbed.
long_description: Originally orchestrated in n8n, then rewritten in Python to test parallel AI workflows — a rewrite that broke as much as it taught. Supabase and React back a discovery surface I reused rather than rebuilt once cost constraints reshaped the project, shifting focus to generating discovery paths from incomplete systems.
outcome: An instructive, unfinished experiment that reframed cost failure as an orchestration problem. At least, until I get back to it...
image_alt: Underfoot travel planner interface showing AI-orchestrated discovery of hidden travel destinations

blog_posts.title: Underfoot: The Chatpot for Hidden Places and Why I Don't Do Hackathons
The Hackathon I Swore Off and the Exhaustion That Mostly Compiled

name: Echo ESLint
what_it_is: A reusable ESLint configuration built for AI-assisted codebases.
why_it_exists: One shared lint boundary that keeps AI agents inside the lines across every repo.
long_description: Echo collapses formatting and linting into a single shared config so the same boundaries apply across projects and agents without restating preferences. Built for AI consumers first, humans second.
outcome: Reduced drift and predictable AI-assisted output, verified with Jest and Sonar.
image_alt: Echo ESLint configuration banner representing reusable linting boundaries for AI coding agents

name: Copilot Chat Extension
what_it_is: A falsified experiment in portable Copilot chat context for IntelliJ teams.
why_it_exists: Tested whether Copilot chat context could survive a jump between IDEs. It couldn't.
long_description: I tried to persist Copilot chat context across environments and found the API simply couldn't support it. Archived immediately — a fast, deliberate kill to avoid sinking effort into a dead end.
outcome: A cleanly falsified hypothesis that redirected effort toward more viable Copilot tooling.
image_alt: Copilot Chat Extension interface showing an experimental portable chat context for IDEs
